team.opens_prefiltered_to: the reader's picked franchise id (team.u8, indexes teams.json) — the sandbox is never blank

preset.blurb: Chennai need nine off Lasith Malinga's final over. Mumbai hold on to win by a single run, the closest final in IPL history.
preset.label: 2019 IPL Final: Mumbai Indians beat Chennai Super Kings by 1 run

scope: R6b full bowl: the field becomes a filterable instrument. The full facet grammar (league, team, season, phase, over-range, outcome, the wicket AND-toggle, batter, bowler, all combinable), the ten-flag guided-tour rail (tourFlags, each resolved and validated against real deliveries so a flag never points at nothing), the tap-a-ball tooltip, and shareable URLs. The team + season facets and the 2019-final preset carry over from the R1b minimal bowl unchanged; the linked worm/Manhattan breakdown panel is the remaining R6b UI surface (blueprint §3, r6b spec §5).

fields.source: the team in matches[arrays.match_index[i]].teams that is not batting_team

tourFlags.blurb: Nine off Malinga's last over, and Mumbai win by one run. All 247 balls of the closest final there's been.
tourFlags.label: The 2019 Final, ball by ball

tourFlags.blurb: 123 balls of India's deadliest yorker at its coolest head. Bumrah's most-bowled duel with anyone.
tourFlags.label: Bumrah's longest duel

tourFlags.blurb: The best single season the IPL has seen. All 655 balls Kohli faced in 2016, the summer he made 973.
tourFlags.label: Kohli's record 2016

tourFlags.blurb: Every maximum in the WPL, from the very first swing to now. 705 of them.
tourFlags.label: Every six in WPL history

tourFlags.blurb: 228 times a side has posted 200 batting first. Here's all 28,704 balls of it, up to the record 287.
tourFlags.label: The 200 Club

tourFlags.blurb: The first season after the mega-auction reshuffle. All 912 wickets that fell across IPL 2022.
tourFlags.label: Every wicket of IPL 2022

tourFlags.blurb: Overs 16 to 20 in the Impact Player era. 4,470 fours and sixes launched at the death since 2023.
tourFlags.label: Death-overs carnage, 2023 to now

tourFlags.blurb: 3,920 sixes in the first six overs, and the modern game nearly doubled the rate, from 1,106 back then to 1,934 now.
tourFlags.label: The powerplay six explosion

tourFlags.blurb: When the bowlers slam the door. 19,414 dot balls squeezed out in the last five overs.
tourFlags.label: The death-overs dot storm

tourFlags.blurb: The WPL's biggest hitter, in full. All 778 balls Shafali Verma has faced, 53 of them sixes.
tourFlags.label: Shafali Verma in the WPL
